bids.reasoning: As this is the opening offer at the very beginning of the negotiation, I will start by proposing my most preferred values for all issues. This serves to maximize my potential utility and sets a strong initial anchor from which I can steadily make considered concessions in subsequent rounds.

The negotiation has just started (time fraction 0.02) and the opponent has countered with a bid that maximizes their utility at the cost of mine. To show a willingness to negotiate while prioritizing my highest-weighted issues, I will maintain my positions on issueD (weight 0.4), issueC (weight 0.3), and issueB (weight 0.2) and make a minor concession on issueA (weight 0.1). This step starts the concession process cautiously.

We are very early in the negotiation (t = 0.04) and the opponent has simply repeated their opening offer of all least-preferred values for me. To keep the negotiation moving, I will incrementally concede on my lowest-priority issue (issueA) while holding firm on my most important issues (issueC and issueD) to protect my overall utility and signal my core priorities.

The time fraction is still very early (t=0.06). The opponent has made a marginal concession on issueC but remains very anchored on their preferred end of the spectrum. I will continue to make small concessions on my less critical issues (issueB) while holding firm on my most valuable issues (issueC and issueD) to encourage them to make more meaningful moves.

The time is still very early (t=0.08). The opponent has begun making small concessions on issueC, raising their offer from valueA to valueC. Since issueC is highly important to me (weight 0.3), this is a positive signal. I will maintain my requests for valueF on my most heavily weighted issues (issueD and issueC) and make another small concession on my least important issue (issueA) to demonstrate flexibility and encourage further concessions from the opponent.

The negotiation is still in its early stages (t=0.1). I will match the opponent's recent concession on `issueA` with `valueB`, as this issue has the lowest importance for me (weight 0.1). I will maintain my top values on my highest priorities (`issueC` and `issueD`) to protect the core of my utility while showing a willingness to find common ground on less critical issues.

The negotiation is still in its early stages (t=0.12). I can see the opponent is making small concessions, exploring different issues like issueC and issueD. Since issueA is my lowest priority (weight 0.1), I will concede entirely on issueA to valueA to demonstrate a willingness to compromise, while continuing to hold extremely firm on my most important issues, issueC and issueD.

In the early phases of this negotiation (t=0.14), I will maintain high utility targets overall while finding areas of overlap based on the opponent's previous offers. The opponent recently proposed `valueE` for `issueC`, which yields high utility for me, so I will match them on `valueE` for `issueC` to signal cooperation. I will hold firm on `issueD` (my highest priority) and maintain my position on `issueB`, where my weight is second-highest.

The opponent has repeatedly insisted on valueA for issueA and low values for issueB, while showing some flexibility on issueC (offering up to valueE) and issueD (offering up to valueD). I will continue to anchor on issueD, my most important issue, while matching their previous valueE on issueC and keeping issueA at valueA. I will make a small concession on issueB to valueD to keep the negotiation moving forward while maintaining an overall utility above 0.80.

The negotiation is still early (t=0.18), so I will maintain a high target utility while continuing to make measured concessions to find a middle ground. The opponent has conceded on issueC (to valueE) and issueD (to valueC), which are issues that hold moderate to high importance for me. Recognizing their flexibility, I will accept their proposal on issueA and issueC, but I'll make a minor concession on issueB (from valueD to valueC) while continuing to hold firm on my most important issue (issueD: valueF).

The opponent's latest offer yields a utility of ~0.70 to me by conceding heavily on my highest priority issues (issueC to valueF and issueD to valueE). Since it's still early in the negotiation (t=0.20), I can seek a higher utility while showing a willingness to trade off issues. I will accept their concession on issueA and offer their previously acceptable value for issueB (valueB), while asking for valueF on my most critical issues, issueC and issueD, targeting a utility of 0.79.

The opponent's most recent offers show a willingness to concede on my most important issues, `issueC` and `issueD`, such as offering `valueF` and `valueE` respectively at `t=0.20`, though they subsequently pivoted to prioritizing `issueB` by presenting `valueD`. I will target ~0.79 utility by synthesizing these signals: conceding completely to `valueA` on `issueA` and adopting their recently requested `valueD` for `issueB`, while requesting high-utility values on my high-weight issues (`issueC` and `issueD`) which they have previously demonstrated a willingness to accept.

The negotiation is still relatively early (t=0.24). I aim to maintain a high utility around 0.76 while strategically accommodating the opponent's demonstrated preferences. I am combining elements from their past offers (issueC: valueF and issueD: valueE from t=0.20, matching their recent issueB: valueC from t=0.24, and keeping issueA: valueA which they frequently request) to present a mutually beneficial package.

I am targeting a utility around 0.71 to continue a steady concession strategy in the early-mid game (t=0.26). The opponent has repeatedly proposed valueA for issueA and valueC for issueB, and shown flexibility on issueD towards valueE, so I will adopt these values to find common ground. I will concede slightly on issueC (from valueF to valueE) to signal cooperation while still preserving utility in my more strongly weighted issues.

The opponent's offers have shown flexibility across various issues, particularly proposing `issueC: valueF` and `issueD: valueE` at `t=0.20`, which gives me a solid utility. I will construct a bid targeting a utility of ~0.73 by accommodating their preferences for `issueA` and recovering their own past proposal for `issueC` and `issueD`. We have plenty of time (`t=0.28`), so I'll yield on `issueB` incrementally to find a compromise.

The current time is early-mid negotiation (t=0.3). The opponent's offers consistently demand my low-priority issues A and B, repeatedly asking for valueA. I will concede entirely on my least important issues (A and B) to valueA, which they clearly prefer, but I will maximize my top priority issues C and D by holding them at valueF. This targets a solid utility of 0.76 while making a substantial, targeted concession on their preferred issues.

The current time is early-mid game (t=0.32). I have identified that my opponent is willing to accept valueF and valueE to my most valuable issues (issueC and issueD) in exchange for concessions on issueA and issueB (e.g., opponent's offer at t=0.20). Because my utility weight for issueA and issueB is small, I will continue making generous concessions on those issues while firmly demanding valueF or valueE on issueC and issueD to maintain my total utility around 0.77.

The negotiation is in the early-mid stage (t=0.34). I will maintain focus on retaining high-utility values on my most important issues (issueD and issueC) while offering concessions on my lower-weight issues (issueA and issueB). The opponent has shown willingness to accept issueA: valueB/valueC and issueB: valueA/valueB, so I will adjust those to find common ground while proposing an offer targeting ~0.76 utility.

The current time fraction is 0.36. I will continue making concessions on my lower-priority issues (issueA and issueB), where the opponent seems to strongly prefer values A and B. I am targeting an overall utility around 0.77 by holding firm on my most valuable issues, issueC and issueD, aiming to find the right acceptable combination.

I am targeting a utility around 0.74, keeping aware that time is progressing but still early enough (t=0.38) to find ideal tradeoffs. The opponent has previously shown willingness to offer `valueF` for `issueC` and `valueE` for `issueD` (e.g., at t=0.20 and t=0.36), which are my highest-weighted issues. I will accept lower values for `issueA` and `issueB` (both set to `valueB`) while standing firm on favorable terms for my critical issues.

The opponent's latest offer includes `valueE` for `issueD` and `valueB` for `issueB`, showing they are willing to concede on my most important issue and are comfortable with `valueB` for `issueB`. Targeting a utility around 0.75 given `t=0.4`, I will offer their preferred `valueB` for `issueB` and their frequently offered `valueE` for `issueC`, while pulling `issueD` back to `valueF` and `issueA` to `valueB` to probe for different trade-offs.

The opponent's most recent offer (issueD: valueE, issueC: valueD) shows they are willing to concede significantly on the higher-priority issues if they are granted their preferred values on issueA and issueB. At this mid-game stage (t=0.42), I will test an offer that provides valueA for issueA and valueB for issueB (their likely priorities), while asking for valueF on my most critical issue (issueD) and valueE on issueC, securing a strong personal utility of roughly 0.74.

The opponent's offers consistently fixate on issueA and issueB (frequently offering valueA for both), while significantly varying issueC and issueD. This tells me they highly value issueA and issueB, which are my lowest-weighted issues. To find a mutually acceptable tradeoff as we approach the mid-game, I will fully concede issueA and issueB to valueA in exchange for my highly valued issueD at valueF and issueC at valueE, targeting a utility of around 0.71.

The current time is roughly halfway to the deadline, allowing me to focus on creating mutually beneficial trade-offs rather than severe concessions. The opponent has previously shown a willingness to accept `issueA: valueC` and `issueB: valueA` (e.g. at t=0.32). I will offer exactly these values for the lower-weight issues while holding firm on maximum utility for my heavily weighted issues, `issueC` and `issueD`, keeping my utility near 0.8.

We are approaching the midpoint (t=0.48). The opponent consistently insists on values A/B/C for issues A and B in their recent offers while occasionally conceding on issues C and D. Since issues C and D are my top priorities, I will offer a strong concession on my lower-weight issues by setting issue A to value B and issue B to value A, while keeping issue D at my top value F and C at value E to maintain a reasonable utility for myself (~0.72).

The current time is at 0.50, and the opponent has consistently favored `issueA: valueA` and `issueB: valueA`. Meanwhile, they have occasionally conceded on `issueC` and `issueD`. To push towards an agreement without dropping my utility too low, I will offer their most preferred values for my two lowest-weighted issues (A and B) while maintaining high-utility values on my most crucial issues (C and D).

I am at the midpoint of the negotiation (t=0.52). The opponent frequently requests valueA or valueB on issueA and issueB. To maximize my utility while making a reasonable concession, I will offer valueB on issueA and valueA on issueB, which they have previously shown interest in. I will pair this with valueF on issueC and valueE on issueD, which are high-utility values for me that the opponent has occasionally included in their past combinations.

The opponent and I are converging on `valueB` for `issueA` and `issueB`, and `valueE` for `issueD`, as seen in their most recent offer. I will accept these values, as they still yield a high baseline utility for me. To ensure my overall utility remains strong (given the approaching deadline), I will hold firm on `valueF` for `issueC`, which the opponent has conceded to in earlier rounds (e.g., at t=0.20, t=0.36, t=0.48).

The opponent's most recent offer includes several values that are favorable enough to me on issues of lower importance (`issueA: valueB`, `issueB: valueA`, `issueC: valueE`). Since we are in the middle of the negotiation (t=0.56), I will concede on these three issues to directly match their last proposal. However, I will hold firm on my most important issue, `issueD`, demanding my top value (`valueF`) to maintain a target utility of around 0.73.

The opponent's recent bid included issueD: valueE, showing some flexibility on my most important issue. By offering valueB for both issueA and issueB, I am conceding on the lower-weight issues where the opponent frequently focuses. I will target issueC: valueE and issueD: valueF to keep my utility reasonably high (around 0.75) as we pass the midpoint of the negotiation.

The negotiation is currently at t=0.60. The opponent's most recent offer strongly signaled a preference for issueA=valueB, issueB=valueB, and issueD=valueE. Given the time pressure, I will concede to match their exact terms on issues A, B, and D, while holding firm on issueC=valueF. This yields a strong utility of 0.741 for me, while aligning with three out of four values from their latest bid.

The current time is 0.62, and we have entered the mid-to-late phase of the negotiation. The opponent has previously shown receptiveness to `valueE` for `issueD` and occasionally `valueF` for `issueC`, as well as options like `valueB` and `valueC` for `issueB`. I am structuring an offer that concedes on my lower-weighted issues (A and B) while maintaining high utility for my top priorities (C and D) to target a utility near 0.77.

The negotiation has passed the halfway mark, and the opponent has shown some flexibility by periodically exploring valueE for my highest-weighted issueD when my value for issueC is lowered. I'll maintain my firm stance on issueD (valueF) and issueC (valueE) since they hold the most utility for me, while continuing to concede on issueA and issueB, aligning with values the opponent has previously proposed (e.g., A=C and B=B). This targeted concession aims to secure a high-utility agreement without relinquishing my core priorities.

The opponent's recent offers strongly prefer valueC for issueA and valueA for issueB. Since these are my two lowest-weighted issues (0.1 and 0.2), I will concede entirely on them to match the opponent's latest requests. In exchange, I will request valueF for issueC and valueE for issueD, which are my highest-valued issues and which the opponent has separately shown willingness to accept in previous rounds. This maintains a strong utility for me (0.728) as we cross the 65% time mark.

The opponent has just proposed an offer that gives me my absolute top preference on both of my highest-weight issues (issueC and issueD, both at valueF). This offer yields a total utility of 0.76, which is an extremely favorable outcome for me. Given that we are now at t=0.68, I will reciprocate their exact bid to secure this excellent agreement before the deadline.
